headline: GOLD NEAR $4,878: Mild Gains Friday as Strait of Hormuz Stays Open During Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire
snippet: Gold extended weekly gains Friday, rising above $4,850/oz as investors reacted to the Strait of Hormuz remaining open under an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire; silver also edged higher with analysts flagging another significant supply deficit ahead as precious-metals volatility eases from recent peaks.

headline: 50 TARGETS DEFINED: Harvest Gold Maps 32km Mineralized Corridor at Mosseau, Eyes 2026 Drill Campaign
snippet: Harvest Gold (TSXV: HVG) defined 50 drill targets along the 32km Kiask River Mineralized Corridor at its Mosseau/LaBelle property in Quebec's Urban Barry Belt, selecting 20 priority targets for its 2026 drill campaign — 11 of them within 2km of the February discovery hole that returned 105 g/t gold over 1.15m.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES: Silver Demand Defies Gravity as Gold-Silver Ratio at 100 Flashes Buy Signal
snippet: China's silver imports topped 790 tonnes in early 2026 driven by solar manufacturing, while the gold-to-silver ratio touching 100 has historically preceded sharp silver outperformance — a setup analysts say mirrors the 2020 COVID reversal that launched silver from $12 to $30 in under a year.

headline: 10,000 METRES MOBILIZED: Fredonia Mining Starts Argentine Gold Drill at El Dorado-Monserrat
snippet: Fredonia Mining (TSXV: FRED) commenced a 10,000-metre diamond drill program at its flagship El Dorado-Monserrat gold project in Argentina's Deseado Massif — one of South America's most prolific orogenic gold belts and host to operating mines including Cerro Negro.

headline: LA RONGE EXPANSION: Trident Resources Options 3,586ha Adjacent to Contact Lake in Saskatchewan Gold Belt
snippet: Trident Resources (TSXV: ROCK) signed a property option agreement for up to 100% of 3,586 hectares in Saskatchewan's La Ronge Gold Belt, directly adjacent to its Contact Lake gold project — adding multiple base and precious metal showings to its 2026 drill pipeline.

headline: LUNDIN MINING Q1 PREVIEW: $10M Mark-to-Market Loss on Gold Collars; Full Results Due May 6
snippet: Lundin Mining (TSX: LUN) flagged an ~$10M unrealized loss on gold collar derivative contracts and a $22M provisional pricing uplift in Q1 2026, ahead of full earnings May 6; a $4M gain from the Eagle mine disposal rounds out the key non-cash items investors should model.

headline: COPPER SURGING: Metal Hits 2-Month High at $6.07/lb on US-Iran Ceasefire Optimism and EV Demand
snippet: Copper futures climbed to $6.07–6.10/lb this week — a two-month high — as US-Iran diplomatic progress raised hopes for uninterrupted Hormuz shipping and structural demand from EV manufacturing and data-center buildouts continues to outpace constrained mine supply.

headline: CAMECO GAINS 4.2%: Uranium Long-Term Price Indicator Rises to $93/lb as Utility Contracting Returns
snippet: Cameco (NYSE: CCJ) rose 4.2% on April 15 as TradeTech's long-term uranium price indicator climbed to US$93/lb, reflecting firmer utility contracting activity after a prolonged period of under-buying — reinforcing the supply-tightness thesis for uranium equities heading into summer.

headline: $75M IPO FILED: Rare Earths Americas Targets NYSE American for U.S.-Brazil Heavy REE Portfolio
snippet: Rare Earths Americas filed an S-1 with the SEC to raise up to $75M on the NYSE American (ticker: REA), led by Cantor Fitzgerald, to advance the Shiloh heavy rare earth project in Georgia and two projects in Brazil — targeting dysprosium and terbium critical to high-performance permanent magnets.

headline: SILVER AT $76 SUPPORT: Asian Metals Update Calls for New Wave Higher in Copper, Non-Ferrous Metals
snippet: Monday's Asian Metals Market Update pegs silver support at $76.20 with resistance near $100, and argues that aluminum's recent outsized rally is pulling copper and nickel higher — a historical pattern that has preceded sustained multi-month non-ferrous metal runs.

headline: 78% IN ONE MONTH: China's Battery VAT Rollback Sends Lithium Carbonate to Highest Since Late 2023
snippet: China's VAT export rebate reduction on lithium batteries — from 9% to 6% effective April 1, 2026 — triggered a producer front-loading rush that sent lithium carbonate futures on the Guangzhou exchange up 78% in just over a month, reaching their highest level since late 2023 before easing slightly.
